analysis.problem: Improper waste sorting and management of plastic waste
analysis.root_cause: Lack of technical skills for managing hazardous waste, insufficient infrastructure, and inadequate education on proper waste sorting
leverage_points: Government and waste management organizations improving waste sorting infrastructure and education programs
Individuals and communities adopting proper waste sorting practices and promoting environmental awareness

proposed_actions.action: Implementing standardized waste sorting protocols and educating the public through awareness campaigns
proposed_actions.measurable_goal: Increase proper waste sorting rates by 20% within the next 12 months

proposed_actions.action: Investing in research and development of new technologies for efficient plastic waste management
proposed_actions.measurable_goal: Reduce plastic waste in landfills by 15% within the next 24 months

evidence: The present world is now facing the challenge of proper management and resource recovery of the enormous amount of plastic waste
Improper waste sorting harms the environment and hinders recycling
analysis.generalization: This problem connects to the domains of Planet, Human, and Civilization, as it affects the health of the biosphere, human well-being, and the overall sustainability of civilization
analysis.summary: Improper waste sorting and management of plastic waste pose a significant problem, requiring immediate attention and action from individuals, communities, and organizations to mitigate its environmental impacts

raw_items.title: EREF Auction Raises More Than $4 Million to Advance Waste and Recycling Research
raw_items.summary: The Environmental Research & Education Foundation (EREF) raised over $4 million during its 2026 Annual Charitable Auction, held during the inaugural Waste Leadership Summit in Washington, D.C.

raw_items.title: Casella’s Roadmap for Renewable Energy and Driving a Circular Economy
raw_items.summary: In this Waste360 Q&A, Casella CEO Ned Coletta catches us up to speed on facility projects and shares what his team learned from testing electric trucks in the real world. He takes us behind the scenes of some exciting partnerships, including work with the University of Vermont to advance a circular economy.

raw_items.title: June 2026 Business Report—Business Highlights from the Waste Leadership Summit
raw_items.summary: In this edition of the Business Report, we recap the highlights of the Investor Summit, as well as several of the education sessions from the Waste Leadership Summit.

raw_items.title: Meet the 2026 Waste360 Women Who Inspire
raw_items.summary: Waste360 is proud to announce its fourth annual Women Who Inspire award winners. Our program, sponsored by Wastequip, recognizes incredible women who are making a difference across waste, recycling, and sustainability.

raw_items.title: Environmental Impact of Improper Waste Sorting
raw_items.summary: Improper waste sorting harms the environment and hinders recycling.Think beyond your bin: Encourage others to sort their waste and treat public spaces as part of the shared environment, because small actions add up to a cleaner future for everyone.

raw_items.title: Impact of Improper Waste Sorting on Environment and Health...
raw_items.summary: How waste sorting helps the environment. When we sort, we allow materials to be reused. Fewer new resources need to be extracted, there’s less pollution, and less waste ends up in landfills. It’s a simple action with a huge impact.

raw_items.title: Frontiers | The impact of improper waste disposal on human health...
raw_items.summary: Environmentalists’ concern about improper waste disposal’s impact on human health and the environment has prompted studies in the United Kingdom.
